name: Testability/DI Pattern Extractor
short_description: Identifies the dependency injection container strategy, mocking framework choice, and protocol-based vs library-based testability approach per module, enabling agents to understand how to write new tests and where seams exist for mocking in a multi-library crypto codebase.
tech_stack: SwiftSyntax — detects: protocol declarations used as injectable abstractions, @Environment / @EnvironmentObject injection sites, Swinject Container.register / resolve call sites, Factory Container.register patterns, Needle Component subclass declarations
Harmonize (perrystreetsoftware/Harmonize, MIT, 2024) — Swift architectural rule: every ViewModel initializer must accept protocol parameters (not concrete types)
Konsist (LemonAppDev/konsist, Apache-2.0, 2024-2025) — Kotlin: every ViewModel constructor must accept interfaces or abstract types; Hilt @HiltViewModel + @Inject constructor presence check
detekt (custom RuleSet) — Kotlin: Hilt @Module + @Provides/@Binds detection, Koin module { factory/single } block scanning, manual constructor injection shape analysis
semgrep — cross-language: MockK (io.mockk) import detection in test files, Mockito usage, Swift MockingKit / Cuckoo framework detection via import statements, XCTest expectation-based mock detection
ast-grep — structural: Kotlin interface mock subclass pattern, Swift protocol-conforming struct/class used only in test targets
tree-sitter (Kotlin + Swift grammars) — low-level token scanning for DI container call patterns when AST visitors insufficient
entity_model: Produces :DIStrategy nodes (container_kind∈{Hilt|Koin|Manual|Swinject|Factory|Needle|EnvironmentObject|None}, language, scope∈{module|project}, test_seam_quality∈{high|medium|low|unknown}) with :GOVERNS edge to :Module. :MockingApproach nodes (framework∈{MockK|Mockito|protocol-manual|Cuckoo|MockingKit|XCTest-manual|None}, language, test_target) with :USED_IN edge to :Module. :InjectableAbstraction nodes (symbol_id, kind∈{protocol|interface|abstract-class}, impl_count, mock_count). :IMPLEMENTS edge from :File to :InjectableAbstraction, :MOCKS edge from test :File to :InjectableAbstraction. :TESTABILITY_SCORE property on :Module. Faceted axes: structural/semantic-kind=Convention, domain-concept=Testability, capability=:Audits.

prior_art.name: Konsist
prior_art.description: Konsist can enforce that every ViewModel class has exactly one constructor whose parameters are interfaces or abstract types. Active 2024-2025, Apache-2.0.

prior_art.name: Harmonize
prior_art.description: Swift architectural linter using SwiftSyntax; can enforce that every ViewModel initializer parameter is a protocol type. MIT, Summer 2024.

prior_art.name: Swinject
prior_art.description: Runtime DI framework for Swift/iOS. Container.register/resolve call pattern is reliably detectable via AST. MIT, active.

prior_art.name: Factory (hmlongco/Factory)
prior_art.description: Compile-time safe Swift DI framework. Uses Swift property wrappers, detectable via @Injected/@LazyInjected patterns.

prior_art.name: Needle (uber/needle)
prior_art.description: Uber's compile-time safe Swift DI via code generation. Component subclass declarations are reliably detectable. MIT.

prior_art.name: MockK
prior_art.description: Kotlin mocking library with mockk() and every/verify DSL. Import detection in test source sets is deterministic. Apache-2.0.

prior_art.name: Google Hilt / Dagger
prior_art.description: Hilt annotations (@HiltViewModel, @Inject, @Module, @Provides) are unique tokens detectable via tree-sitter/semgrep with 100% precision.

consumer_query_patterns.label: What DI strategy does each iOS module use?
consumer_query_patterns.cypher: MATCH (d:DIStrategy {language:'swift'})-[:GOVERNS]->(m:Module) RETURN m.name, d.container_kind, d.test_seam_quality ORDER BY m.name

consumer_query_patterns.label: Find all protocol seams that have no mock implementation
consumer_query_patterns.cypher: MATCH (ia:InjectableAbstraction {mock_count: 0}) RETURN ia.symbol_id, ia.impl_count ORDER BY ia.impl_count DESC LIMIT 20

consumer_query_patterns.label: Which modules have low testability scores?
consumer_query_patterns.cypher: MATCH (m:Module) WHERE m.testability_score < 40 RETURN m.name, m.testability_score, m.platform ORDER BY m.testability_score ASC

consumer_query_patterns.label: Which mocking framework is used across all test targets?
consumer_query_patterns.cypher: MATCH (ma:MockingApproach)-[:USED_IN]->(m:Module) RETURN ma.framework, ma.language, count(m) AS module_count ORDER BY module_count DESC

graphiti_episode_type: EntityNode — :DIStrategy and :MockingApproach are persistent per-module facts with valid_from. :InjectableAbstraction nodes are EntityNodes tracking impl_count and mock_count as evolving properties. CommunityNode clusters modules sharing identical DI strategies.
ingest_perf_est: [uncertain] Full ingest: ~12–18 min on 350K LOC. Incremental: ~45–90 s. Hilt annotation detection is near-instant (tree-sitter token scan).
failure_modes: Manual DI without any framework produces ~75% precision via protocol parameter shape heuristic
SwiftUI @EnvironmentObject may be used for non-DI purposes (theme objects) — inflates DI score
Hilt kapt/KSP code generation: generated Dagger components are not in source tree
Mixed DI strategies: per-context disambiguation needed
Protocol-vs-class detection: protocols sometimes used as namespaces
Kotlin Koin vs manual: Koin DSL looks similar to manual Map-based DI
Swift @Observable macro (Swift 5.9+): changes EnvironmentObject detection accuracy
open_questions: Should :InjectableAbstraction nodes link to :Symbol nodes via a :CORRESPONDS_TO edge, or are they the same node type?
test_seam_quality scoring formula: derived from mock_count/impl_count, coverage data, or multi-factor?
Needle Component hierarchy is rich graph — extract subgraph or just strategy classification?
For KMP shared modules, classify as 'kotlin' platform or separate 'kmp-shared'?
Testability score: 0-100 cardinality or categorical enum to avoid false precision?
adversarial_resistance: medium — framework import detection is deterministic. Protocol-shape heuristics bypassable via 'Protocol' suffix on concrete type without actual protocol.
